description: Rust progressive 1080p 420: 30899 µs. C libjpeg-turbo: 30358 µs. Delta: +541 µs (+1.8%). Baseline (non-prog): Rust 11109 µs, C 11376 µs. Progressive overhead: 19790 µs (2.78x). Profiled hotspots: Huffman table resolution inside inner loops, is_multiple_of modulo per MCU, no fast AC combined path in progressive AC first, per-block unwrap() calls.

Hoist table resolution + countdown restart + fast AC combined path + unsafe ZIGZAG + split non-interleaved loops. 1080p 420: 30899→29611 µs (-4.2%). Now 2.5% faster than C (29611 vs 30358). All 5 progressive sizes beat C by 2-3%. Baseline unaffected.

Branchless apply_correction_bit using arithmetic shift for sign. 1080p 420: 29611→30058 µs (+1.5% regression). Branch predictor handles the sign check efficiently since most coefficients are positive in practice. Reverted.

Batch correction bit reads in EOB path using peek_bits(16) + manual bit extraction. 1080p 420: neutral (+0 µs). 64-bit bit buffer already makes individual read_bits(1) fast. Reverted for simpler code.
Enable fast AC combined path for all al values (not just al=0). Also deduplicate Huffman decode paths in decode_ac_first — merged fast+slow paths. Code simplification with neutral performance.
